long_stay: Full-time programs at universities and university colleges (Bachelor’s, Master’s, or PhD programs)
Preparatory courses leading to such higher education
qualifying_courses.notes: Distance learning or part-time programs do not qualify for a residence permit.

booking_methods.description: Online application for the Residence Permit for Studies.
In-person appointment required for passport verification and biometric enrollment (photo and fingerprints) after submitting the online application.

steps.title: Secure admission
steps.description: Obtain admission via the national application portal (universityadmissions.se).

steps.title: Pay first tuition fee installment
steps.description: Pay the first tuition fee installment directly to your university.

steps.title: Submit online application
steps.description: Submit an online application through the Swedish Migration Agency (Migrationsverket) portal.

steps.title: Complete passport verification and biometrics
steps.description: Attend an appointment at a designated Swedish Embassy or Consulate-General to show your original passport and complete biometric enrollment.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: High-quality color scans of all pages containing stamps, text, or visual details, including blank pages and covers.

required_documents.name: Official Notification of Selection Results (Admission Letter)
required_documents.details: Proving full-time acceptance to an accredited higher education provider.

required_documents.name: Proof of Tuition Fee Payment
required_documents.details: Receipt or confirmation showing payment of the first tuition installment.

required_documents.name: Comprehensive Health Insurance
required_documents.details: Required if your course lasts less than one year.
exemptions: Students with programs longer than one year (automatically integrated into the Swedish healthcare system)

required_documents.name: Detailed Financial Documentation
required_documents.details: Showing sufficient funds for the entire requested duration of your permit.

acceptable_proof: Bank statements in your own name from an open checking/savings account
financial_requirements.notes: Sponsorship letters or bank statements belonging to third parties (like friends or extended relatives) are generally not accepted unless they are formal institutional funds or bank accounts under your absolute legal control.

residence_permit.notes: The Residence Permit Card (UT-kort) is issued after approval. If a visa is required, the card is collected at the embassy before travel. For visa-exempt countries, the card is printed locally in Sweden after arrival. Address notification to Migrationsverket within 30 days of arrival is mandatory. Registration at the Swedish Tax Agency (Skatteverket) for a personal identity number (personnummer) is required if the course lasts more than one year.

restrictions: Must maintain acceptable academic progress regardless of work schedule
Working more than 15 hours per week during term time can lead to the revocation or non-renewal of your permit

required_documents: Updated transcripts from the Ladok system (proving at least 37.5 credits in the first year and 45 credits per year for subsequent years)
New bank statement showing the maintenance fund for the upcoming year

requirements: Successfully completed at least two semesters of higher education program (for transition to work permit)
Employment offer meets national collective agreement standards and minimum salary thresholds (for work permit)
post_study_work.notes: Graduates can apply for a specialized residence permit to look for work or start a business for 12 months. After securing a qualifying job offer, they can apply for a Work Permit from within Sweden.

official_sources.name: Study in Sweden
official_sources.description: Official Guide to Higher Education in Sweden

common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Upload high-quality color scans of every single page of your passport, including blank pages and covers.
Achieve at least 37.5 credits during your first year of study, and at least 45 credits per year for any subsequent years; ensure you are passing your courses before applying for renewal.

common_mistakes.mistake: Using Third-Party Bank Accounts for Financial Proof
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Funds must be in a bank account under your own name; sponsorship letters from private individuals are generally not accepted unless they are formal institutional funds or bank accounts under your absolute legal control.
